summary: Pristine soundboard recording with a standout Scarlet>Fire second set, though the first set feels thin and occasional gating issues soften the mix.
review: The two‑track SBD capture is remarkably clear – many reviewers call it “sharp” and “gorgeous” – but the heavy noise‑gate on some instruments makes vocals and keyboards sound distant. Musically the show shines in the second half, especially the Scarlet>Fire sequence, Here Comes Sunshine and Baba O’Riley, while the short first set and songs like So Many Roads receive mixed reactions.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the recording’s clarity and the second‑set performances, but a sizable minority note gating, drop‑outs, and a weak first set; the weighted average of show (70 %) and recording (30 %) yields a solid above‑average rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix praised as “pristine” by several users; however, heavy noise‑gate makes keyboards and vocals sound weak or clipped, and a few reviewers note occasional drop‑outs in the drum/space segment.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is short and considered uneven, but the second set builds momentum with a powerful Scarlet>Fire transition, a heartfelt HC Sunshine, and extended jams that carry the show. Some reviewers note an odd placement of HC Sunshine and a rough transition into Fire.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia is lauded for his tone, runs on HC Sunshine and Corrina, and overall fine form, though a few users mention weak vocals and occasional lack of focus later in the set.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil Lesh receives glowing praise – called “shiznit” and a “big maniacal rubber spider” – with many noting his bass work as a highlight of the night.
band_member_comments.Keys: The keyboard mix suffers from heavy noise‑gating, making instruments sound distant; Vince’s playing on Music Never Stopped is singled out positively.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drummers are described as giving a funky feel with bongos and providing solid comping, but some feel the drum/space segment lacks energy.
